payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because mem_new was judged to be the superseding (typically newer or higher-priority) record for that same person/affiliation combination. mem_old was filtered out since it represented an older version of the same fact and therefore was treated as superseded by mem_new.
payload.decision: Prefer mem_new over mem_old because mem_new is considered the superseding/most recent version for the same underlying entity and attribute set (Sam at Goldman).

entity_resolution.reason: Both mem_new and mem_old refer to the same person identity ('Sam') and the same affiliation ('Goldman'), so the system treats them as conflicting versions of the same fact.

payload.intent: Explain the recall conflict and why the system returned mem_new for Sam (Goldman) while filtering out mem_old as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: mem_old: Sam — Goldman (earlier/older version). Filtered because it is superseded by mem_new.

details: Classified the conflict as supersession between mem_new and mem_old for the same entity+relationship (Sam ↔ Goldman).
Justified filtering mem_old as superseded and selecting mem_new as the preferred version.

repair_options: If mem_old should remain, mark it with a non-superseding status or adjust the recency/priority rule that selects mem_new.
Add disambiguating details to mem_old (e.g., time range or role) so it is not considered the same fact as mem_new.
Ensure entity resolution distinguishes between different 'Sam' individuals if applicable (different IDs/names).
